That screen is coming from the Settings page trying to load /api/settings, and it’s getting a 404. 

In your backend, /api/settings is a real route, but the server runs on port 8787.  

So the 404 usually means your frontend is asking the Vite server (5173) for /api/settings instead of your backend, or the backend is not running at all.

Do this in order

1. Confirm the backend is actually running
   Open this in your browser:
   [http://localhost:8787/api/health](http://localhost:8787/api/health)

If that does not return JSON, start the app using the script that runs both servers. Your package.json is set up for that. 
Run:
npm run dev

2. If [http://localhost:8787/api/health](http://localhost:8787/api/health) works, fix the dev proxy so /api goes to 8787
   Edit vite.config.ts to include a proxy like this, then restart npm run dev:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  server: {
    proxy: {
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://localhost:8787',
        changeOrigin: true
      }
    }
  }
})
```

After restart, refresh the app and the System page should stop showing HTTP 404 because /api/settings will be forwarded to the backend route. 

One more gotcha
If you are using vite preview (or opening a built/static frontend), the Vite proxy is not active. In that case you must either run a real reverse proxy, or serve the dist folder from the express server so the frontend and /api share the same origin.

Keep UI styling unchanged.

A) In server/index.ts there is a broken merge object in POST /api/settings
It currently does:
const merged = { .current, .newSettings };
Replace with:
const merged = { ...current, ...newSettings };

B) Any server file importing types from ../types.ts must use type only imports because interfaces and types do not exist at runtime in Node ESM.
Replace
import { AppSettings } from '../types.ts'
with
import type { AppSettings } from '../types.ts'

Apply to at least:
server/extractor.ts
server/queue.ts
server/storage.ts

export const API_BASE = import.meta.env.DEV ? 'http://localhost:8787
' : '';

fetch(${API_BASE}${url}, init)

components/Settings.tsx
